name: Systemic Weather
description: A world-state weather system where conditions transition over simulation time and affect visibility, movement, resources, AI, audio, or survival pressure.
design_notes: Systemic weather should be more than a visual layer. It needs state ids, transition rules, regional or biome masks, gameplay modifiers, forecast cues, save restoration, and accessibility settings for visibility, audio, and flashing or storm effects.
design_purpose: Make the world feel dynamic through conditions that affect play and planning.
Connect environment, survival, economy, AI, and strategy decisions through readable weather changes.
Give designers a pacing tool for exposure, travel, combat, production, or population effects.
player_fantasy: The player feels the world has changing conditions that can be predicted, endured, exploited, or avoided.

parameters.name: weather_state_id
parameters.typical_range: clear, rain, storm, snow, heatwave, fog
parameters.description: Current weather condition applied to a region or world.

parameters.name: weather_transition_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule that chooses the next weather state.

parameters.name: simulation_clock_id
parameters.description: Clock source used for transition checks and forecasts.

parameters.name: biome_id
parameters.description: Biome or region profile that constrains weather options.

parameters.name: forecast_window_ms
parameters.description: Lookahead window shown to players or AI planners.

parameters.name: weather_effect_profile_id
parameters.description: Gameplay modifier bundle applied by the current weather state.

parameters.name: save_snapshot_id
parameters.description: Persisted weather state, forecast queue, and transition progress.

edge_cases: The player saves during a transition between weather states.
Weather affects visibility but accessibility settings increase scene brightness.
A biome transition crosses two incompatible weather profiles.
Offline simulation advances weather past several queued forecasts.
Networked clients enter the area after the authoritative weather transition already occurred.
common_bugs: Visual rain starts before gameplay wet-road or exposure modifiers are active.
Weather transition RNG is not saved and changes after reload.
Forecast UI reads planned state while gameplay uses current weather_state_id.
Particle and audio effects continue after moving to a biome where the state is invalid.
Weather updates every agent individually and exceeds performance budgets in large worlds.
balancing_notes: Weather penalties should be telegraphed before they invalidate plans or routes.
Frequent transitions make the world lively but can obscure player cause and effect.
Forecast reliability can be a strategic resource when uncertainty is communicated clearly.
accessibility_notes: Provide reduced storm flash, reduced wind noise, and visibility assist settings.
Keep critical UI and subtitles readable during fog, rain, snow, or darkness effects.
Offer clear forecast text for players who cannot parse subtle sky or audio cues.

implementation_notes.unity: Represent weather_state_id and forecast queues in a weather manager driven by simulation_clock_id. Apply visual, audio, physics, and gameplay modifiers from the same committed state, and persist transition progress in save_snapshot_id.
implementation_notes.godot: Use an autoload weather controller or world Resource to own state transitions. Scene nodes for particles, shaders, lighting, and audio should subscribe to weather state changes rather than choosing their own conditions.
implementation_notes.unreal: A world subsystem can own weather transitions, regional tags, and replicated weather_state_id. Gameplay effects, audio, Niagara systems, and post-process volumes should branch from the same profile id for consistency.
implementation_notes.web: Keep weather state in world simulation data and render effects as a projection. Use deterministic transition rules with saved RNG state so refreshes and server-client reconciliation preserve forecasts and current conditions.

relationships.reason: Weather state can feed temperature exposure, shelter value, and wetness rules.
Wind force can express weather state through gameplay movement and projectile effects.
Weather visibility effects need contrast-safe cues and non-color warnings.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
example_games.note: Uses weather to affect traversal, combat, and visibility.

example_games.title: Frostpunk
example_games.note: Uses weather and temperature pressure as part of city survival.
